req.title: Hr-Admin deletes expense-claim
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to delete a expense-claim through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-043 covers UI-driven deletion of an expense-claim by an hr-admin. Three risk areas drive test selection: (1) happy-path deletion with audit verification (EP – valid partition), (2) unauthenticated and forbidden-role rejections (NEG/DT – error pathways), (3) boundary on the claim identifier – a single-character / minimum-length id to probe edge input handling (BVA). A state-transition case covers the full lifecycle: claim exists → delete initiated → claim absent + audit present (STC). Together these satisfy the minimum positive/negative/boundary requirement while targeting the explicit error codes (400, 401, 403) and success outcomes (200/201, entity id, audit record) stated in the requirement.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_deletes_expense_claim_successfully
preconditions: A valid expense-claim record exists in the system
actions: Navigate to /expense-claims
Locate the target expense-claim row by its id
Click the Delete action for that row
Confirm the deletion in the confirmation dialog
Capture the HTTP response and the resulting UI state
Query the audit log for the deletion record
expected: Response body contains the deleted entity id
Deleted claim no longer appears in the claims list
An audit record referencing the claim id and action=DELETE exists
trace: R-WEB-043 – audit record written on success
R-WEB-043 – response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_hr_admin_deletes_expense_claim_successfully(
    hr_admin_page,          # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright page for hr-admin
    create_expense_claim,   # <fixture>: creates a claim, yields its id, cleans up if not deleted
    audit_log_client,       # <fixture>: thin wrapper around audit-log API
    app_base_url,           # <fixture>: e.g. "https://hr-app.local"
    api_response_interceptor, # <fixture>: captures last XHR response
):
    claim_id = create_expense_claim()
    page = hr_admin_page

    page.goto(f"{app_base_url}/expense-claims")
    page.locator(f"[data-claim-id='{claim_id}'] [data-action='delete']").click()
    page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete-btn']").click()

    response = api_response_interceptor.last()
    assert response["status"] in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status {response['status']}"
    assert str(claim_id) in str(response["body"])

    expect(page.locator(f"[data-claim-id='{claim_id}']")).not_to_be_visible()

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.fetch(entity_id=claim_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "No audit record found for deletion"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_expense_claim
preconditions: A valid expense-claim id is known
actions: Attempt to send a DELETE request to /expense-claims/{id} without auth credentials
Capture the HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an authentication error message
No audit record is written for this attempt
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_expense_claim(
    app_base_url,           # <fixture>
    create_expense_claim,   # <fixture>
    audit_log_client,       # <fixture>
):
    claim_id = create_expense_claim()

    response = requests.delete(
        f"{app_base_url}/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        headers={},  # no auth header
        timeout=10,
    )

    assert response.status_code == 401, (
        f"Expected 401, got {response.status_code}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    assert <helper_fn>.has_error_key(body), "Response missing error indicator"

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.fetch(entity_id=claim_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, "Audit record must NOT be written for unauthenticated attempt"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_expense_claim
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role: employee (not hr-admin)
A valid expense-claim id is known
actions: Authenticate as a user with role employee
Attempt to send a DELETE request to /expense-claims/{id} with employee credentials
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a forbidden/authorization error
No audit record is written for this attempt
trace: R-WEB-043 – error pathway: 403 if role forbidden
R-WEB-043 – precondition: role must be hr-admin
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_expense_claim(
    app_base_url,               # <fixture>
    create_expense_claim,       # <fixture>
    employee_auth_headers,      # <fixture>: auth headers for role=employee
    audit_log_client,           # <fixture>
):
    claim_id = create_expense_claim()

    response = requests.delete(
        f"{app_base_url}/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        headers=employee_auth_headers,
        timeout=10,
    )

    assert response.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403, got {response.status_code}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    assert <helper_fn>.has_forbidden_key(body), "Response missing forbidden indicator"

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.fetch(entity_id=claim_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, "Audit record must NOT be written for forbidden role"

tests.name: test_delete_expense_claim_with_minimum_length_id
preconditions: An expense-claim whose id is exactly at the minimum allowed length exists
actions: Construct a DELETE request using the minimum-length claim id
Submit the request with hr-admin credentials
Capture HTTP response status and body
Query audit log for the deletion record
expected: Response body contains the minimum-length claim id
Claim is no longer retrievable via GET /expense-claims/{id}
An audit record for the deletion exists
trace: R-WEB-043 – boundary: minimum-length entity id accepted
R-WEB-043 – audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

MIN_ID_LENGTH = 1  # BVA lower bound for claim id

def test_delete_expense_claim_with_minimum_length_id(
    app_base_url,               # <fixture>
    hr_admin_auth_headers,      # <fixture>
    create_expense_claim,       # <fixture>
    audit_log_client,           # <fixture>
):
    claim_id = create_expense_claim(id_length=MIN_ID_LENGTH)
    assert len(str(claim_id)) == MIN_ID_LENGTH, "Fixture did not return minimum-length id"

    del_resp = requests.delete(
        f"{app_base_url}/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        headers=hr_admin_auth_headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert del_resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200/201, got {del_resp.status_code}"
    )
    assert str(claim_id) in del_resp.text

    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{app_base_url}/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        headers=hr_admin_auth_headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 404, "Claim should be absent after deletion"

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.fetch(entity_id=claim_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Audit record missing for minimum-length id deletion"

tests.name: test_expense_claim_lifecycle_state_transition_on_delete
preconditions: No prior deletion of the target claim has occurred in this session
actions: Create a new expense-claim (state: ACTIVE)
Verify claim is retrievable – confirm state ACTIVE
Delete the claim via the UI form at /expense-claims
Verify claim is no longer retrievable – state transitions to DELETED
Attempt to delete the same claim a second time
Observe the response for the duplicate-delete attempt
expected: First DELETE returns 200 or 201 with entity id
Claim transitions from ACTIVE to DELETED/absent
Audit record is written after first deletion
Second DELETE attempt returns 404 or 409 (claim no longer exists)
No duplicate audit record is written for the second attempt
trace: R-WEB-043 – idempotency: second delete does not re-audit
R-WEB-043 – audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_expense_claim_lifecycle_state_transition_on_delete(
    app_base_url,               # <fixture>
    hr_admin_auth_headers,      # <fixture>
    create_expense_claim,       # <fixture>
    audit_log_client,           # <fixture>
):
    claim_id = create_expense_claim()

    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{app_base_url}/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        headers=hr_admin_auth_headers, timeout=10,
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200, "Claim should be ACTIVE before deletion"

    del1 = requests.delete(
        f"{app_base_url}/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        headers=hr_admin_auth_headers, timeout=10,
    )
    assert del1.status_code in (200, 201)
    assert str(claim_id) in del1.text

    audit_after_first = audit_log_client.fetch(entity_id=claim_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_after_first) == 1, "Exactly one audit entry expected after first delete"

    del2 = requests.delete(
        f"{app_base_url}/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        headers=hr_admin_auth_headers, timeout=10,
    )
    assert del2.status_code in (404, 409), (
        f"Second delete should return 404/409, got {del2.status_code}"
    )

    audit_after_second = audit_log_client.fetch(entity_id=claim_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_after_second) == 1, "No new audit record should appear for duplicate delete"
